title: aira capillaris subsp. corsica
intro: Aira capillaris subsp. corsica, also known as Corsican Hairgrass, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and providing relief from respiratory problems. Aira capillaris subsp. corsica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed and utilized in a variety of ways.
